You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.175 (commit: 05a5e229).

## Issue to Resolve

[BUG]: Edit Note Preview does not show the expected card
### Checked for duplicates?

- [X] This issue is not a duplicate

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

open the browser
Select a card which is not card 1 of the note
long press
in the menu, tap "edit note"
Tap preview

### Expected behaviour

You should see the selected card in the preview mode (or at least, the card should be consistent with the card underlined at the bottom of the editor)

### Actual behaviour

First card of the note is shown (At least I assume. In the case I was interested in, I saw the "this card is empty" warning)

### Debug info

```text
2.17 alpha 3
```


### (Optional) Anything else you want to share?

_No response_

### Research

- [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app)
- [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue
- [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting))

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

This will be slightly easier to fix once https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/14719 goes in:

* `ordinal` is provided to the editor: 
* `ordinal` is `ord`, NOT the index into the array
  * `{{c1::AA}} {{c3::BB}}` returns the second ordinal as 2

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L84

`mOrdinal` is not used to set `templateIndex` in this branch:

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L233-L241

`setCurrentCardFromNoteEditorBundle` uses `templateIndex = 0` and displays the wrong card

I've added two regression tests to help guide a fix

One undefined question: what should the note editor previewer show if one of the cards is empty? Should it be skipped, or displayed

Should this be different between:
* Card Template Preview
* Note Editor Preview
* Card Browser Preview (less likely to want to see empty cards)

For when a few dependencies have been merged:

```kotlin
    private fun indexFromOrdinal(col: Collection, ordinal: Int): Int {
        return when (mEditedNotetype?.isCloze) {
            true -> col.clozeNumbersInNote(col.newNote(mEditedNotetype!!)).indexOf(mOrdinal)
            else -> ordinal
        }
    }
```
Is that correct? with c1 and c3, that will return [1,3], and ord 3 will be an index out of bounds, won't it?

Edit: sorry, ignore me - I mistook this for numbers[ord] instead of searching for the given value.
In the note editor preview, there seems to be no specific card. So I don’t understand what sense it would make to have this behavior on the note editor preview.

For card template preview, I certainly believe you should see the preview for the card you are looking it.

For the browser, if the card selected in the browser is empty, I believe you should show that the card is empty. That’s an information I certainly may want to have if it is the case. Because either:
* there was no recent "clean card"
* there was a clear card, and this is card 1, generated in order to ensure the note is not deleted. 

The first case may be interesting, the second is certainly something that should be noted.

Also, if we touch this part of the code, it may be worth to give more context, by stating either:
* This card is empty. The n cards of this note are empty.
* This card is empty. Jump to the first non-empty card. (with a link/button)

And I guess, potentially, a checkbox to skip the empty card when browsing which cards will be generated.


If you agree it makes sense, I assume it should be many different bugs so they can be easily tackled

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Collection.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

Now I understand the issue. Let me analyze it:

1. When opening the note editor preview from a card in the browser that is not card 1, the preview shows card 1 instead of the selected card.

2. Looking at the code in `CardTemplatePreviewer.kt`:
   - `mOrdinal` is received from the intent parameters (line 88)
   - But in `onCollectionLoaded`, when `mNoteEditorBundle != null`, the code calls `setCurrentCardFromNoteEditorBundle(col)` (line 238)
   - In `setCurrentCardFromNoteEditorBundle`, it uses `cardIndex` (line 278) which is initialized to 0 and never set from `mOrdinal`

3. The issue is that when loading from the note editor bundle, `cardIndex` starts at 0 and is not initialized from `mOrdinal`.

4. The fix should convert the `mOrdinal` (which is the card's `ord` value) to the appropriate `cardIndex`.

Looking at the discussion:
- `ordinal` is `ord`, NOT the index into the array
- For cloze types: `{{c1::AA}} {{c3::BB}}` returns the second ordinal as 2 (not 1)
- `indexFromOrdinal` function should handle this conversion

Looking at existing helper functions:
- `Note.cardIndexToOrd(index: Int): Int` - converts index to ord
- We need the inverse: `ordToCardIndex(ord: Int): Int`

For regular notes: ord == index
For cloze notes: we need to find the index of the given ord in the list of cloze numbers

Let me look at how to properly set the initial `cardIndex` from `mOrdinal`.

Now I understand the issue. Let me analyze the code flow:

1. When opening the note editor preview, `mOrdinal` is passed from the intent (line 88)
2. However, in `onCollectionLoaded`, when `mNoteEditorBundle != null`, the `cardIndex` is used (which starts at 0)
3. The `cardIndex` is never initialized from `mOrdinal`

The fix needs to convert `mOrdinal` (which is the card's `ord` value) to the correct `cardIndex`. For cloze notes, this is different because cloze ordinals can have gaps (e.g., `{{c1::A}} {{c3::B}}` has ordinals [0, 2] but indices [0, 1]).

Let me look at how the ordinal is passed and used, and also check how `cardIndexToOrd` works since we need the inverse:

package com.ichi2.anki

import android.content.Intent
import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.NoteService
import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.NoteService.getFieldsAsBundleForPreview
import org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers.containsString
import org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers.not
import org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
import org.junit.Test
import org.junit.runner.RunWith
import org.robolectric.RobolectricTestRunner

/**
 * Tests for Issue 14694: Edit Note Preview does not show the expected card
 * 
 * When a user selects a card that is not card 1 of the note and opens the preview,
 * the preview should show the selected card, not the first card.
 */
@RunWith(RobolectricTestRunner::class)
class CardTemplatePreviewerOrdinalTest : RobolectricTest() {

    /**
     * Test that the ordinal provided is used for standard note types.
     * When editing a note with a "Basic (and reversed card)" type and selecting the second card,
     * the preview should display the second card (which shows "World" on the front).
     */
    @Test
    fun `ordinal provided shows correct card for standard note type - Issue 14694`() {
        val fields: MutableList<NoteService.NoteField?> = arrayListOf(
            Field(0, "Hello"),
            Field(1, "World")
        )

        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW).apply {
            val basicModel = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy("Basic (and reversed card)")
            val tempModelPath = CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(targetContext, basicModel)
            putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModelPath)
            putExtra(
                "noteEditorBundle",
                bundleOf(
                    "editFields" to getFieldsAsBundleForPreview(fields, false),
                    "did" to 1L
                )
            )
            putExtra("ordinal", 1) // Select the second card (reversed card)
        }

        val testCardTemplatePreviewer = super.startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent)

        // The second card (reversed) has "Back" template that shows field 1 on the front
        assertThat("Front field should not be displayed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardContent, not(containsString("Hello")))
        assertThat("Back field should be displayed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardContent, containsString("World"))
    }

    /**
     * Test that the ordinal provided is used for cloze note types.
     * With cloze text "{{c1::Hello}} {{c3::World}}", ordinal 2 corresponds to c3 (the second card).
     * The preview should show the c3 card which has "Hello" visible and "World" hidden.
     */
    @Test
    fun `ordinal provided shows correct card for cloze note type - Issue 14694`() {
        val fields: MutableList<NoteService.NoteField?> = arrayListOf(
            Field(0, "{{c1::Hello}} {{c3::World}}"),
            Field(1, "Extra")
        )
        // For cloze with c1 and c3: c1 has ord=0, c3 has ord=2
        val ordinalOfSecondCard = 2

        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW).apply {
            val clozeModel = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy("Cloze")
            val tempModelPath = CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(targetContext, clozeModel)
            putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModelPath)
            putExtra(
                "noteEditorBundle",
                bundleOf(
                    "editFields" to getFieldsAsBundleForPreview(fields, false),
                    "did" to 1L
                )
            )
            putExtra("ordinal", ordinalOfSecondCard)
        }

        val testCardTemplatePreviewer = super.startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent)

        // For c3 card: Hello is visible (c1 is shown), World is hidden (c3 is the cloze deletion)
        assertThat("c1 text should be visible (Hello)", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardContent, containsString("Hello"))
        assertThat("c3 text should be hidden (World)", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardContent, not(containsString("World")))
    }

    /**
     * Test that navigation buttons have correct state when starting at a non-first card.
     * When ordinal is provided to select the second card, the previous button should be enabled.
     */
    @Test
    fun `navigation buttons have correct state when starting at non-first card - Issue 14694`() {
        val fields: MutableList<NoteService.NoteField?> = arrayListOf(
            Field(0, "Hello"),
            Field(1, "World")
        )

        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW).apply {
            val basicModel = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy("Basic (and reversed card)")
            val tempModelPath = CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(targetContext, basicModel)
            putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModelPath)
            putExtra(
                "noteEditorBundle",
                bundleOf(
                    "editFields" to getFieldsAsBundleForPreview(fields, false),
                    "did" to 1L
                )
            )
            putExtra("ordinal", 1) // Start at second card
        }

        val testCardTemplatePreviewer = super.startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent)

        // Starting at second card: previous should be enabled, next should be disabled
        assertThat("Previous button should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled())
        assertThat("Next button should be disabled", !testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled())
        assertThat("Card index should be 1", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex == 1)
    }

    private inner class Field(override val ord: Int, private val text: String) : NoteService.NoteField {
        override val fieldText: String
            get() = text
    }
}
"}

content.text:
The fix is complete. Let me provide a summary of the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
When a user selects a card that is not card 1 of a note (e.g., from the card browser), then edits the note and taps preview, the preview incorrectly shows the first card instead of the selected card.

### Root Cause
In `CardTemplatePreviewer.kt`, when loading from the note editor:
- The `ordinal` (card's `ord` value) was passed from NoteEditor via the intent
- However, `cardIndex` (used to determine which card to preview) was always initialized to 0
- The `mOrdinal` value was never used to initialize `cardIndex` when loading from the note editor bundle

### Solution
Made the following changes to `CardTemplatePreviewer.kt`:

1. **Added `mOrdinalProvided` field** (line 48): Tracks whether an ordinal was explicitly provided in the intent, to distinguish between "ordinal=0" being explicitly set vs defaulting to 0.

2. **Updated `onCreate`** (lines 91-96): Detects if ordinal was explicitly provided - checks for key existence when loading from intent, or reads the saved value when restoring from instance state.

3. **Updated `onSaveInstanceState`** (line 235): Saves `mOrdinalProvided` to survive configuration changes.

4. **Updated `onCollectionLoaded`** (lines 247-258): 
   - When ordinal is explicitly provided, converts it to `cardIndex` using the new `getCardIndexFromOrdinal()` function
   - Sets navigation button states correctly based on the initial `cardIndex`

5. **Added `getCardIndexFromOrdinal()` function** (lines 326-353): 
   - For standard note types: cardIndex = ordinal
   - For cloze note types: finds the index of the ordinal in the list of cloze numbers (handles gaps like `{{c1::...}} {{c3::...}}`)

### Testing
- All existing tests pass (14 tests)
- Created new test file `CardTemplatePreviewerOrdinalTest.kt` with 3 tests to verify:
  - Ordinal works correctly for standard note types (e.g., "Basic (and reversed card)")
  - Ordinal works correctly for cloze note types with gaps (e.g., c1 and c3)
  - Navigation buttons are properly enabled/disabled when starting at a non-first card

549→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
550→        // cards without notes

552→            return false
553→        }
554→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
555→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
556→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
557→        ) > 0
558→        // notes without cards or models
559→        if (badNotes) {

561→        }
562→        // invalid ords
563→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
564→            // ignore clozes
565→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
566→                continue
567→            }
568→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model

574→                m.getLong("id")
575→            ) > 0
576→            if (badOrd) {

578→            }

package com.ichi2.anki

import android.content.Intent
import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.NoteService
import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.NoteService.getFieldsAsBundleForPreview
import org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers.containsString
import org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers.not
import org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
import org.junit.Test
import org.junit.runner.RunWith
import org.robolectric.RobolectricTestRunner

/**
 * Tests for Issue 14694: Edit Note Preview does not show the expected card
 * 
 * When a user selects a card that is not card 1 of the note and opens the preview,
 * the preview should show the selected card, not the first card.
 */
@RunWith(RobolectricTestRunner::class)
class CardTemplatePreviewerOrdinalTest : RobolectricTest() {

    /**
     * Test that the ordinal provided is used for standard note types.
     * When editing a note with a "Basic (and reversed card)" type and selecting the second card,
     * the preview should display the second card (which shows "World" on the front).
     */
    @Test
    fun `ordinal provided shows correct card for standard note type - Issue 14694`() {
        val fields: MutableList<NoteService.NoteField?> = arrayListOf(
            Field(0, "Hello"),
            Field(1, "World")
        )

        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW).apply {
            val basicModel = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy("Basic (and reversed card)")
            val tempModelPath = CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(targetContext, basicModel)
            putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModelPath)
            putExtra(
                "noteEditorBundle",
                bundleOf(
                    "editFields" to getFieldsAsBundleForPreview(fields, false),
                    "did" to 1L
                )
            )
            putExtra("ordinal", 1) // Select the second card (reversed card)
        }

        val testCardTemplatePreviewer = super.startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent)

        // The second card (reversed) has "Back" template that shows field 1 on the front
        assertThat("Front field should not be displayed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardContent, not(containsString("Hello")))
        assertThat("Back field should be displayed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardContent, containsString("World"))
    }

    /**
     * Test that the ordinal provided is used for cloze note types.
     * With cloze text "{{c1::Hello}} {{c3::World}}", ordinal 2 corresponds to c3 (the second card).
     * The preview should show the c3 card which has "Hello" visible and "World" hidden.
     */
    @Test
    fun `ordinal provided shows correct card for cloze note type - Issue 14694`() {
        val fields: MutableList<NoteService.NoteField?> = arrayListOf(
            Field(0, "{{c1::Hello}} {{c3::World}}"),
            Field(1, "Extra")
        )
        // For cloze with c1 and c3: c1 has ord=0, c3 has ord=2
        val ordinalOfSecondCard = 2

        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW).apply {
            val clozeModel = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy("Cloze")
            val tempModelPath = CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(targetContext, clozeModel)
            putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModelPath)
            putExtra(
                "noteEditorBundle",
                bundleOf(
                    "editFields" to getFieldsAsBundleForPreview(fields, false),
                    "did" to 1L
                )
            )
            putExtra("ordinal", ordinalOfSecondCard)
        }

        val testCardTemplatePreviewer = super.startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent)

        // For c3 card: Hello is visible (c1 is shown), World is hidden (c3 is the cloze deletion)
        assertThat("c1 text should be visible (Hello)", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardContent, containsString("Hello"))
        assertThat("c3 text should be hidden (World)", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardContent, not(containsString("World")))
    }

    /**
     * Test that navigation buttons have correct state when starting at a non-first card.
     * When ordinal is provided to select the second card, the previous button should be enabled.
     */
    @Test
    fun `navigation buttons have correct state when starting at non-first card - Issue 14694`() {
        val fields: MutableList<NoteService.NoteField?> = arrayListOf(
            Field(0, "Hello"),
            Field(1, "World")
        )

        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW).apply {
            val basicModel = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy("Basic (and reversed card)")
            val tempModelPath = CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(targetContext, basicModel)
            putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModelPath)
            putExtra(
                "noteEditorBundle",
                bundleOf(
                    "editFields" to getFieldsAsBundleForPreview(fields, false),
                    "did" to 1L
                )
            )
            putExtra("ordinal", 1) // Start at second card
        }

        val testCardTemplatePreviewer = super.startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent)

        // Starting at second card: previous should be enabled, next should be disabled
        assertThat("Previous button should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled())
        assertThat("Next button should be disabled", !testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled())
        assertThat("Card index should be 1", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex == 1)
    }

    private inner class Field(override val ord: Int, private val text: String) : NoteService.NoteField {
        override val fieldText: String
            get() = text
    }
}
"}
